items.headline: Bascom Avenue traffic signal project gets consultant contract extension
items.summary: City Council approved a third amendment to extend the consultant agreement with DKS Associates for the Bascom Avenue Highway-Rail Traffic Signal Project. This infrastructure project aims to improve traffic and safety at the rail crossing on Bascom Avenue.

items.headline: City awards contract for local street pavement repairs
items.summary: City Council approved bids and awarded a contract for the 2026 Local Streets Remove and Replace Pavement Project. This project will address deteriorating pavement conditions on residential streets across San José.

items.headline: Cupertino updates Active Transportation Plan with revised scoring
items.summary: The city presented Phase 2 of its Active Transportation Plan on April 7, including updates to the plan and new scoring criteria for evaluating projects. This plan shapes how the city prioritizes biking, walking, and transit infrastructure investments.

items.headline: Council vacancy ordinance being changed
items.summary: The City Council introduced an ordinance to amend how council vacancies are handled, though specific details of the changes aren't clear from the agenda. Residents should watch for what this means for how future empty council seats get filled.

items.headline: Major senior housing complex approved for East St John
items.summary: Trillium is building a 65-unit senior apartment complex at 678 E St John St for $8.8 million. This represents significant new affordable housing for San José's aging population.

items.headline: Garden Gate Coalition lawsuit against city proceeds
items.summary: The city council met in closed session on April 1 to discuss existing litigation with the Garden Gate Coalition over Mary Avenue Safety (Case No. 26CV488611 in Santa Clara Superior Court). This ongoing dispute involves residents challenging city decisions on a local street.

items.headline: City approves gas rate changes for 2027 budget year
items.summary: The City Council approved updates to residential and commercial natural gas rates for fiscal year 2027. Rates for G-1 (residential), G-2 (commercial), and G-3 (large commercial) service are being adjusted.

items.headline: City launches business amnesty program and retail incentives
items.summary: On March 27, the council discussed a Business License Amnesty Program targeting shopping centers and new Economic Development Incentives aimed at attracting retail, small businesses, and startups to Cupertino. The city is also running a Summer of Celebrations Shop Local campaign.
